title: Dense Mexican Duck Tostada
description: A malty, chewy, dairy-free Mexican dish featuring shredded duck, coconut-tamarind sauce, and crispy tostada shells, served in an earthenware bowl.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C and bake the tostada shells for 5 minutes until crisp.
Season the duck thigh with salt, pepper, and olive oil, then bake in the same oven at 180°C for 25 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 75°C.
Let the duck rest for 5 minutes, then shred the meat with two forks.
Heat the salsa adobo in a small saucepan for 3 minutes.
In a bowl, combine the shredded duck, coconut milk, tamarind paste, and the warmed salsa adobo, stirring until well mixed.
Transfer the duck mixture to an earthenware bowl and arrange the crisp tostada shells on top.
Sprinkle chopped cilantro and pickled red onion over the shells.
Drizzle lime juice across the tostada for a fresh finish.
